title: Sentence Skills and Writing Practice Test

questions.questionText: Which is a complete sentence?
questions.explanation: A sentence needs a subject (dog) and verb (runs).

questions.questionText: Arrange: school / to / I / go / every / day
options: I go to school every day.
Go I to school every day.
I every day go to school.
School I go to every day.
questions.explanation: I go to school every day is the correct order.

questions.questionText: Which sentence has correct punctuation?
questions.explanation: Questions end with a question mark.

questions.questionText: Arrange: likes / Ravi / to / read / books
questions.explanation: Ravi likes to read books is the correct order.

questions.questionText: Which word should start with a capital letter?
questions.explanation: India is a proper noun and needs a capital letter.

questions.questionText: Choose the correct sentence.
questions.explanation: With 'she', we use 'doesn't' and the base form 'like'.

questions.questionText: Arrange: the / cat / is / sleeping / under / table / the
options: The cat is sleeping under the table.
Cat the is sleeping under table the.
The cat sleeping is under the table.
Under the table the cat is sleeping.
questions.explanation: The cat is sleeping under the table is correct.

questions.questionText: Which sentence needs an exclamation mark?
questions.explanation: What a beautiful day! shows strong feeling.

questions.questionText: Arrange: playing / children / are / in / the / park
options: Children are playing in the park.
Playing children are in the park.
The park children are playing in.
Are children playing in the park.
questions.explanation: Children are playing in the park is correct.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is a question?
questions.explanation: Where is my bag? asks something and ends with ?

questions.questionText: Arrange: my / name / is / Priya
questions.explanation: My name is Priya is the correct order.

questions.questionText: Which sentence has a mistake?
questions.explanation: Three pencil should be three pencils (plural).

questions.questionText: Arrange: eat / I / breakfast / morning / every
questions.explanation: I eat breakfast every morning is correct.

questions.questionText: Which is a command or request?
questions.explanation: Please help me is a request.

questions.questionText: Arrange: blue / is / the / sky
questions.explanation: The sky is blue is the correct order.

questions.questionText: Which sentence uses 'their' correctly?
questions.explanation: Their shows ownership. That is their book.

questions.questionText: Arrange: birthday / is / today / my
questions.explanation: Today is my birthday is correct.

questions.questionText: Which sentence needs a full stop?
questions.explanation: I love my family is a statement and needs a full stop.

questions.questionText: Arrange: water / drink / we / should / enough
questions.explanation: We should drink enough water is correct.

questions.questionText: Which sentence uses correct grammar for 'he'?
questions.explanation: He doesn't like tea is correct.

questions.questionText: Arrange: flowers / garden / in / the / are / beautiful
options: The flowers in the garden are beautiful.
Flowers the in garden the are beautiful.
Beautiful are the flowers in the garden.
In the garden the flowers are beautiful.
questions.explanation: The flowers in the garden are beautiful is correct.

questions.questionText: Which word completes: ___ am a student.
questions.explanation: I am a student. I refers to the speaker.

questions.questionText: Arrange: mother / my / cooks / food / delicious
questions.explanation: My mother cooks delicious food is correct.

questions.questionText: Which sentence has correct capitalization?
questions.explanation: I and Delhi need capital letters.

questions.questionText: Arrange: sun / rises / the / in / east / the
options: The sun rises in the east.
Sun the rises in the east.
The sun in the east rises.
Rises the sun in the east.
questions.explanation: The sun rises in the east is correct.
